title: leucorchis albida subsp. straminea
intro: Leucorchis albida subsp. straminea, also known as the gold-scaled white orchid, is a rare and endangered terrestrial orchid species native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Leucorchis albida subsp. straminea include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential side effects.
